message:
test(golden): tighten remaining Display/Debug assertions into exact-match contracts

Second pass over the Display/Debug test surface, converting the
last few `contains`-based smoke checks into exact-golden equality
assertions so any format-string drift fails loudly instead of sliding
through as long as the substring is still present somewhere.

crates/ffs-error/src/lib.rs:
- FfsError::Parse Display: freeze full "parse error: insufficient
  data: need 4 bytes at offset 0, got 2" string.
- CorruptMetadata Display: assert the exact
  "corrupt metadata at block 18446744073709551615: out of range"
  line instead of checking only that u64::MAX appears.
- MvccConflict Display: freeze the "MVCC conflict: transaction …
  conflicts on block …" form including both u64 extremes.
- From<io::Error>: freeze "I/O error: file gone".
- Unicode NotFound: freeze "not found: 日本語ファイル.txt".
- RepairFailed Debug: freeze the full `RepairFailed("…")` form.
- ModeViolation / RepairFailed Display: freeze the full prefixed
  strings instead of just substring-matching on the detail.
- io::Error source(): freeze the inner "inner cause" message.

crates/ffs-types/src/lib.rs:
- ParseError::InsufficientData, InvalidMagic, IntegerConversion
  Display strings are now frozen in full, and an explicit exact
  test is added for InvalidField to cover that variant too.
- DeviceId zero-padded Display is now compared against the exact
  32-char hex literal rather than probed by length + suffix.

crates/ffs-repair/src/scrub.rs:
- ScrubFinding Display (checksum_mismatch and bad_magic) and
  ScrubReport Display (corrupt-path and io_error-path) now compare
  against exact golden strings including severity, tag, and counts.

crates/ffs/src/lib.rs:
- DetectionError::UnsupportedImage Display is now frozen against
  the full "image does not decode as supported ext4/btrfs
  superblock" message.

.beads/issues.jsonl: flush closure records for the eight tracked
Display/Debug golden tightening tasks in ffs-error, ffs-types,
ffs-repair, and ffs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-22w.7","title":"Add comprehensive unit tests for MVCC conflict resolution","description":"# Add comprehensive unit tests for MVCC conflict resolution

## GOAL
Exhaustive unit test coverage for all MVCC conflict scenarios: FCW, SSI, phantoms, cascading aborts.

## DELIVERABLES
1. FCW tests: concurrent writes to same block, first committer wins
2. SSI tests: read-write antidependency cycles detected
3. Phantom tests: range scan + concurrent insert detected
4. Cascading abort tests: dependent transactions correctly abort
5. GC correctness: pruned versions not accessible
6. Snapshot isolation: readers see consistent state
7. Edge cases: empty transactions, read-only transactions, self-conflict

## TESTS (≥30 individual test cases)
Each test must:
- Document the invariant being tested
- Use descriptive names (test_fcw_same_block_second_writer_aborts)
- Log the scenario setup for debugging

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. ≥30 unit tests for MVCC conflict resolution
2. All FCW/SSI invariants covered
3. No flaky tests (deterministic scheduling via lab runtime)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleElk","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:55:12.868400922Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T20:30:55.690459862Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T20:30:55.690438933Z","close_reason":"Added explicit empty/self-conflict/cascading-abort MVCC tests; validated via rch fmt/check/clippy/test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22w.7","depends_on_id":"bd-22w","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:55:12.868400922Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-22w.7","depends_on_id":"bd-22w.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:55:37.926604108Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-22w.7","depends_on_id":"bd-38k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:55:38.024837031Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":182,"issue_id":"bd-22w.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: concurrency_perf
Mapped graveyard sections: 14.10 EBR + 15.1 S3-FIFO + 15.4 Parallel WAL
EV score: 13.333333333333334 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: S/A
Budgeted mode: max_chain_len=64, wal_flush_batch=256, dirty_ratio_cap=0.8
Fallback trigger: single-writer conservative mode with bounded queue

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-22w.8","title":"Implement MVCC append-only commit log with crash consistency","description":"# Implement MVCC append-only commit log with crash consistency

## GOAL
Persist MVCC version chains and commit decisions via an append-only log so that committed transactions survive restart and in-flight transactions are cleanly aborted on crash recovery.

## BACKGROUND
Currently MVCC state is in-memory only. On restart, all version history is lost. For a real filesystem, committed MVCC state must be durable. The append-only log approach provides:
- Crash consistency: only complete log entries are visible after recovery
- Sequential I/O: append-only pattern is SSD-friendly
- Simple recovery: scan forward from last checkpoint, replay committed, discard uncommitted

## DESIGN

### Log Format
- Fixed-size header: magic, version, checksum_algorithm, head_lsn
- Variable-size log entries, each with:
  - LSN (Log Sequence Number): monotonically increasing u64
  - entry_type: enum { TxnBegin, TxnWrite(block_id, old_version, new_version), TxnCommit(commit_seq), TxnAbort }
  - payload_len: u32
  - payload: serialized entry data
  - crc32c: entry checksum (covers type + payload)

### Commit Protocol
1. TxnBegin written when transaction starts
2. TxnWrite for each block version created
3. TxnCommit atomically makes all writes visible
4. Without TxnCommit, all writes are rolled back on recovery

### Recovery Protocol
1. Open log, find last valid entry (checksum scan)
2. Build set of committed transaction IDs
3. For each TxnWrite: if txn committed, apply to version chain; else discard
4. Truncate log at last valid entry
5. Write recovery evidence record

### Checkpointing
- Periodically write a checkpoint entry containing current version chain state
- On recovery, start from last checkpoint instead of beginning of log
- Checkpoint interval: configurable (default: every 1000 commits or 10MB of log)

### Integration
- TxnManager (ffs-mvcc): write log entries during transaction lifecycle
- Mount path: replay log before serving any FUSE requests
- ffs-block: version chain state restored from log

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Committed transactions survive restart (write, unmount, remount, read)
- [ ] Uncommitted transactions are cleanly aborted on crash recovery
- [ ] Log entries are checksummed; corrupt entries detected and truncated
- [ ] Checkpoint reduces recovery time for long-running filesystems
- [ ] Recovery evidence record logged to evidence ledger
- [ ] Unit tests: write-commit-recover round-trip
- [ ] Unit tests: write-crash-recover (no commit = abort)
- [ ] Unit tests: checkpoint reduces replay range
- [ ] Benchmark: log write throughput (target: 100K entries/sec)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:22:22.617675822Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:59:16.875211492Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T17:59:16.875131942Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crash","mvcc","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22w.8","depends_on_id":"bd-22w","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:22:22.617675822Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-22w.8","depends_on_id":"bd-22w.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:18.954599266Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-22w.9","title":"E2E test: concurrent writers with SSI — no serialization anomalies","description":"# E2E test: concurrent writers with SSI — no serialization anomalies

## GOAL
Prove that FrankenFS MVCC with SSI correctly prevents all serialization anomalies under concurrent writers, including write-skew, phantom reads, and lost updates.

## BACKGROUND
SSI (Serializable Snapshot Isolation) should guarantee that all concurrent transaction schedules produce results equivalent to some serial execution. This test exhaustively verifies that guarantee.

## TEST SCENARIOS

### Scenario 1: Write-Skew Detection
- T1: read block A, write block B based on A's value
- T2: read block B, write block A based on B's value
- Both attempt to commit under SI
- Expected: at least one aborted (rw-antidependency cycle detected)
- Run 1000 iterations with random timing

### Scenario 2: Lost Update Prevention
- T1 and T2 both read block X, compute X+1, write X
- Expected: FCW detects conflict, one aborted, final X = initial+1 (not initial+2 then lost)
- Run 1000 iterations

### Scenario 3: Phantom Read Prevention
- T1: scan range [0..100], compute aggregate
- T2: insert new block in range [0..100]
- T1: re-scan same range
- Expected: T1 sees consistent snapshot (phantom detected if T2 commits first)

### Scenario 4: Multi-Writer Stress
- 8 concurrent threads, each running 100 transactions
- Each transaction: read 3 random blocks, write 2 random blocks
- After all complete: verify database is in a state reachable by SOME serial ordering
- Use deterministic scheduling with configurable seed

### Scenario 5: High-Contention Hot Key
- 16 threads all incrementing the same counter block
- Expected: all increments applied (with retries on abort), final value = 16 * iterations
- Measures: abort rate, retry overhead, throughput

### Verification
- After each scenario: dump version chains, verify no anomalies
- Count aborts: should be non-zero for conflict scenarios
- Timing: log p50/p95/p99 transaction latencies

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Write-skew correctly detected and aborted in 100% of cases
- [ ] Lost updates never occur
- [ ] Phantom reads detected when applicable
- [ ] Multi-writer stress produces serializable results
- [ ] Hot-key scenario completes with correct final value
- [ ] All scenarios deterministic with seed
- [ ] Test completes in < 60s","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:22:40.037282113Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:33:36.770790207Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T18:33:36.770708313Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","mvcc","ssi","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22w.9","depends_on_id":"bd-22w","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:22:40.037282113Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-22w.9","depends_on_id":"bd-22w.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:21.146295713Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-22w.9","depends_on_id":"bd-38k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:20.315321852Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-4ogh","title":"Implement ext4/btrfs parity features: decompression, inline data, bigalloc, ZERO_RANGE","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-22T18:28:47.537528441Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T18:29:02.634096851Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T18:29:02.633525312Z","close_reason":"Implemented 4 new parity features:
(1) btrfs transparent decompression: ZLIB (flate2) + ZSTD in read path for inline and regular extents. Added ram_bytes field to BtrfsExtentData enum. LZO deferred (custom framing).
(2) ext4 inline data read: reads from inode extent_bytes + xattr_ibody when EXT4_INLINE_DATA_FL is set. Removed INLINE_DATA from REJECTED_V1 incompat set.
(3) ext4 bigalloc infrastructure: added cluster_ratio to FsGeometry (cluster_size/block_size for BIGALLOC, else 1).
(4) ext4 ZERO_RANGE fallocate: writes zero blocks to existing allocated extents in range. Mode 0x10 now accepted.
All tests pass (471 ffs-core, 360 ffs-ondisk, 142 ffs-alloc, 117 ffs-btrfs, 114 ffs-extent).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ext4","parity"]}

removed stray 'master' wording; audit rg checks clean","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6dl","depends_on_id":"bd-126","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:13:56.949662975Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6dl","depends_on_id":"bd-1va","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:13:57.023734986Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6dl","depends_on_id":"bd-2ds","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:13:56.500113505Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6dl","depends_on_id":"bd-2l4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:13:56.649526704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6dl","depends_on_id":"bd-3bf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:35:19.506425155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6dl","depends_on_id":"bd-3gt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:13:56.725444543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6dl","depends_on_id":"bd-3h8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:13:56.799958240Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6dl","depends_on_id":"bd-3hi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:13:56.875381332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6dl","depends_on_id":"bd-89x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:35:19.593236853Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6dl","depends_on_id":"bd-hv6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:13:56.574678369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2,"issue_id":"bd-6dl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Initial known doc/code drift to include in the Errata pass (starting point, not exhaustive):

- Cancelled errno mapping mismatch: COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC currently says EINTR; crates/ffs-error maps to ECANCELED.
- Parser error taxonomy needs explicit layering: ffs-ondisk returns ParseError today; user-facing layers return FfsError.
- PLAN references ARCHITECTURE.md and CONVENTIONS.md which do not exist; decide create vs remove.

 {"id":"bd-8rgp","title":"Add btrfs write-path FUSE E2E tests mirroring ext4 coverage","description":"## What
Add btrfs write-path FUSE E2E tests that exercise create/write/mkdir/rename/unlink/truncate/
xattr/fallocate/symlink through the actual FUSE mount interface, mirroring the ext4 FUSE E2E
tests that already exist in fuse_e2e.rs.

## Why
ext4 has comprehensive FUSE E2E tests: fuse_create_and_read_file, fuse_write_overwrite_and_append,
fuse_mkdir_and_nested_create, fuse_unlink_removes_file, fuse_rename_file, fuse_hard_link,
fuse_symlink_create_and_follow, fuse_setattr_truncate — all exercising real kernel VFS through
FUSE mount.

btrfs write operations are tested only at the unit level (OpenFs methods in ffs-core) and in
the crash matrix. There are NO btrfs FUSE E2E write tests that go through the kernel.

## What Already Exists
- ext4 FUSE E2E write tests in crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs (lines 531-673)
- btrfs FUSE E2E read tests exist (mount + read files)
- Helper: with_rw_mount() for ext4 RW FUSE mounts
- btrfs mkfs.btrfs image creation in fuse_e2e.rs (line 1889-1910)
- btrfs unit write tests in ffs-core (create, mkdir, rename, fallocate, etc.)

## Implementation Plan
Add to crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs, using a with_btrfs_rw_mount() helper:

fn fuse_btrfs_create_and_read_file()
  - mkfs.btrfs → mount --rw → create file → read back → verify content → unmount

fn fuse_btrfs_write_and_read_back()
  - mount → create → write 4KB → read → verify → unmount

fn fuse_btrfs_mkdir_and_nested_create()
  - mount → mkdir → create file in subdir → read → verify → unmount

fn fuse_btrfs_rename_file()
  - mount → create → rename → verify new path exists, old doesn't → unmount

fn fuse_btrfs_unlink_removes_file()
  - mount → create → unlink → verify gone → unmount

fn fuse_btrfs_setattr_truncate()
  - mount → create → write 8KB → truncate to 4KB → verify size → unmount

fn fuse_btrfs_xattr_set_get_list_remove()
  - mount → create file → setxattr → getxattr → listxattr → removexattr → unmount

fn fuse_btrfs_fallocate_prealloc()
  - mount → create → fallocate 4096 bytes → verify size → unmount

fn fuse_btrfs_symlink_create_and_follow()
  - mount → create target → symlink → readlink → read through symlink → unmount

### Helper: with_btrfs_rw_mount()
- Create 256MB sparse file
- mkfs.btrfs -f on it
- Mount via FrankenFS FUSE with --rw
- Run test closure
- Unmount
- Skip if mkfs.btrfs or /dev/fuse unavailable

## Considerations
- Tests need both mkfs.btrfs and /dev/fuse — skip gracefully if either missing
- btrfs minimum image ~128-256MB (much larger than ext4 8MB) — use sparse files
- Use --test-threads=1 for FUSE tests to avoid mount contention
- Tests must unmount cleanly even on assertion failure (use Drop guard)
- btrfs write support is experimental — some operations may not be fully wired
  through FUSE yet; tests should document any that return EOPNOTSUPP

## Test Plan
- cargo test -p ffs-harness --test fuse_e2e -- fuse_btrfs --nocapture --test-threads=1
- All tests pass when mkfs.btrfs and /dev/fuse available
- All tests skip cleanly when either is unavailable
- No interference with ext4 FUSE tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 9 btrfs FUSE E2E write-path tests implemented
- [ ] with_btrfs_rw_mount() helper handles setup/teardown
- [ ] Tests skip gracefully without mkfs.btrfs or /dev/fuse
- [ ] All 9 tests pass on systems with FUSE+btrfs-progs
- [ ] Unmount cleanup is reliable (Drop guard)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:46:46.038588133Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T21:23:42.513062175Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T21:23:42.512793045Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: 19 btrfs FUSE E2E tests exist at fuse_e2e.rs:1965-2395 including create, mkdir, rename, unlink, symlink, xattr, fsync, large write","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-idp4","title":"Update CHANGELOG.md to cover commits from 2026-03-21 through current","description":"## What
Update CHANGELOG.md to document all significant commits since the last entry (2026-03-21).
Approximately 60+ commits need documentation, organized by capability area (not chronological).

## Why
CHANGELOG.md covers "2026-02-09 through 2026-03-21" with 536 commits documented. Since then,
major work has landed: btrfs experimental RW support, crash-consistency matrix, fallocate
operations, log contract hardening, per-core FUSE dispatch, evidence pipeline expansion,
tracing flake fixes, and 81+ new tests across 4 crates.

The CHANGELOG serves as the project history for onboarding and as input to the
comprehensive-codebase-report skill. Stale changelog = invisible work.

## Implementation Plan
1. Run: git log --oneline --since="2026-03-21" | wc -l (count commits)
2. Group commits by capability area (following existing CHANGELOG structure):
   - On-Disk Parsing (ext4/btrfs)
   - FUSE Mount and VFS Layer
   - MVCC Concurrency Engine
   - Self-Healing Repair Pipeline
   - Write Path (Allocation, Extents, Inodes, Directories, Xattrs)
   - Journal and WAL Recovery
   - CLI and Observability
   - Conformance Harness and Testing
   - Foundation Types and Error Handling
3. Write entries with commit links: [hexsha](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenfs/commit/hexsha)
4. Update header: "Period covered: 2026-02-09 through 2026-04-06"
5. Update total commit count

## Considerations
- Follow existing style: bullet points grouped by feature area, commit links in brackets
- Don't document individual test additions — summarize as "expanded test coverage for X"
- Include the tracing callsite cache fix as it's architecturally interesting
- Include the btrfs fallocate sector-alignment fix
- Include the per-core metrics test fix
- Don't document build/fmt/cosmetic commits

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All significant commits since 2026-03-21 documented
- [ ] Organized by capability area matching existing structure
- [ ] Commit links included and correct
- [ ] Period and total count updated in header","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-06T15:50:33.040113984Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:34:29.037607885Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T01:34:29.037145379Z","close_reason":"Updated CHANGELOG.md through 2026-04-06 with 134-post-cutoff commit coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-idp4","depends_on_id":"bd-g1ue","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:18:11.676458110Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-p3sd","title":"FUSE E2E: fuse_spec_i5_delete_reclaims_free_counts fails at file 169","description":"The test creates 1000 files in a subdirectory then tries to delete them all. File 169 consistently returns ENOENT during fs::remove_file. File 169 is the first entry in the second directory block (the directory transitions from 1 block to 2 blocks at this point).

Root cause analysis:
- Core-level test (create 1000 + lookup + unlink) passes - the filesystem logic is correct
- The failure is FUSE-specific - kernel dentry cache or FUSE protocol interaction
- The MVCC store correctly holds all committed data
- Adding sync/flush between create and delete phases does not fix it
- The failure is 100% deterministic at file 169

Possible causes:
1. FUSE kernel negative dentry caching interfering with create/unlink sequence
2. Directory extent resolution issue specific to the FUSE lookup path under kernel cache pressure
3. Kernel dentry eviction + daemon lookup returning stale data

Next steps: Add FUSE-level tracing/logging to the lookup and unlink paths to capture the exact sequence of FUSE operations around file 169","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-27T19:06:40.043910040Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T19:37:10.917554076Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T19:37:10.917280702Z","close_reason":"Fixed: checksum tail was not initialised in new directory blocks, causing parse_dir_block to fail with rec_len overflow","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-plyn","title":"Bitmap CRC: cross-validate crc32c crate against software implementation","description":"Write a test that computes CRC32C of the same 4096-byte bitmap data using:
(a) the crc32c crate 0.6.8 (current)
(b) a manual software CRC32C implementation (polynomial 0x1EDC6F41, reflected)
(c) the crc32fast crate (if available) for a third opinion

Compare all three results. If they differ, the crc32c crate has a hardware-acceleration bug on this CPU. If they agree, the bug is in our bridge function or the data we're checksumming.

Test data: a real mkfs.ext4 bitmap block (or a synthetic 4096-byte buffer with known CRC).

ACCEPTANCE: A test that definitively proves whether the crc32c crate produces correct results for 4096-byte inputs on this platform.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:11.470459311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:25:26.728288089Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:25:26.727814833Z","close_reason":"Root cause found: bitmap CRC was chaining group_number + using full block length. Fixed in commit 20d65f4.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crc32c","ext4","investigation"]}

 {"id":"bd-pv9l","title":"e2compr: implement double/triple indirect write and cluster freeing","description":"Extend the indirect write support to double-indirect (i_block[13]) and triple-indirect (i_block[14]) levels. This covers files up to the ext4 maximum.

Also implement cascading free: when all pointers in an indirect block become 0, free the block and propagate the zero to the parent level. This prevents block leaks when large compressed files are deleted or truncated.

DEPENDS ON: Single-indirect write support (bd-z15u).

ACCEPTANCE: Compressed write works for arbitrarily large files. Deleting a compressed file frees all indirect blocks.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:18.026009802Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:53:04.109030647Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:53:04.108567410Z","close_reason":"Added double-indirect (i_block[13]) and triple-indirect (i_block[14]) write support with lazy intermediate block allocation. Commit 06a4288.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2compr","ext4","write_path"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-pv9l","depends_on_id":"bd-z15u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:28.301559286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-zge.8","title":"ffs-core: Integrate ffs-journal replay into ext4 mount path + crash-recovery conformance","description":"Goal: make journal replay observable to users by wiring the new ffs-journal engine into the ext4 open/mount pipeline and proving behavior with deterministic crash-recovery tests.

Scope:
- Integrate replay_jbd2 invocation into ffs-core ext4 open flow behind compatibility-mode guard.
- Define journal-region discovery from ext4 superblock metadata (journal inode/device pointers) with explicit unsupported-mode errors.
- Surface replay metrics (ReplayStats) through engine diagnostics/logging path for auditable mount decisions.
- Add unit tests for integration glue (invocation conditions, error mapping, no-op behavior when journal absent).
- Add harness-level crash/recovery test flow that simulates committed and uncommitted journal transactions and verifies post-mount state.
- Add detailed structured logging lines for replay decisions and outcomes (scanned, committed, revoked, skipped).

Acceptance:
- Mount/open of ext4 fixture with journal transactions replays committed updates and ignores uncommitted/revoked updates.
- cargo test -p ffs-core and cargo test -p ffs-harness -- --nocapture include replay-path coverage with deterministic assertions.
- Logs contain machine-parseable replay summary fields suitable for CI diagnostics.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:21:35.744999433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T06:35:12.471070906Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T06:35:12.471036722Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","semantics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zge.8","depends_on_id":"bd-zge","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:21:35.744999433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-zge.8","depends_on_id":"bd-zge.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:21:35.744999433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
